original_reasoning: Arabic apple and Greek lotus-eater meet in a shared fruit and edible-plant domain, though the match is still indirect.
Arabic denotes lightness and flightiness; Greek plausibly carries a loose/wanton or unstable character nuance. The chain through frivolous instability is workable.
Arabic 'multitude, group' and Greek 'variety of tones' meet in the shared notion of multiplicity.
Piercing leads naturally to the tool or place for pierced gear and weapon storage; action to container is plausible.
Arabic rapid driving and Greek copious pouring both preserve forceful outward movement or discharge; concrete motion survives with drift.

Arabic raja = defer, postpone, hold back, set aside (the Murji'a sect = 'those who defer'). Greek eirgo = close off, enclose, hold back, restrain. Both encode the 'holding back/deferral' core directly. This is exactly the calibration anchor pattern (ara-defer ↔ grc-hold-back). Strong direct link.
The prepared or nominated person comes forward; this gives a plausible bridge to come or go to.
The Arabic gloss is an extra teat above the udder; Greek τελέθω expresses coming forth or growing, giving a plausible growth-based chain.
